column1: # ZAP rule configuration for the baseline and full scans.
# Keep this file intentionally strict. Add ignores only after documenting why
# the alert is accepted for the local unauthenticated login surface.

column3: Next.js local scan responses may intentionally be non-storable; cache policy is handled by the framework and hosting layer.
Next.js requires inline script allowances unless the app adopts nonce-based CSP; tracked as accepted local baseline risk.
Base64-like strings in generated Next.js CSS/JS chunks are false positives, not disclosed secrets.
Modern SPA behavior is expected for this Next.js application.
Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy is not required for the unauthenticated app surface and can break third-party auth/media integrations.
ZAP requests omit browser Sec-Fetch-Dest headers; this is scanner behavior, not an application response defect.
308 Permanent Redirect responses have no body; Content-Type is not required by RFC 7538 and its absence on redirects is a ZAP false positive.
